LAW AND MOTION 301, ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS CONTINUED TO MARCH 7, 2011 AT 9:30 AM IN DEPT. 301 TO BE HEARD BY JUDGE BUSCH PER JUDGE BURDICK'S DECISION. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR # 7489

LAW AND MOTION 301, RULING - A R G U E D; ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE REGARDING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION - THE COURT FINDS IT PROPER TO ISSUE A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE PEOPLE HAVE PROVEN THAT SUFFICIENT NOTICE TO COMPLY WITH THE LAW WERE NOT GIVEN AND THERE IS A PROBABILITY OF SUCESS IN SHOWING THAT OPPOSING DEFENDANTS HAVE PARTICIPATED IN MR. GUAJARDO'S UNAUTHORIZED PRACTICE OF LAW. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER GRANTING PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION BASED ON THE TRANSCRIBED ORDER IN TODAY'S HEARING. THE ORDER IS STAYED FOR 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Criminal Investigation; Declarations Of Steven Gruel And Kristen E. Caverly In Support
Off Calendar Jan-31-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA S. WHITE & ATTY. KRISTINE A. POPLAWSKI, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4259 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. MICHAEL APARICIO OF ORRICK, TEL. NO. (650) 614-7473 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFFS;
ATTY. KRISTEN E. CAVERLY OF HENDERSON CAVERLY & CHARNEY LLP, TEL. NO. (858) 755-3000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS SKENDER AND IPG.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER STENDER AND IMMIGRATION PRACTICE GROUP, P.C.'S Motion To Stay Proceedings Pending Criminal Investigation IS Denied. None of the applicable factors weigh in favor of defendants' request for an open-ended stay of all pre-trial proceedings or alternative request for an open-ended stay of all party discovery. While it may be appropriate to stay specified activities such as Mr. Stender's deposition or to appoint a discovery referee, the parties have not formally requested such relief and, more importantly, do not appear to have met and conferred regarding any discovery plan designed to address Mr. Stender's intention to invoke the Fifth Amendment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 =302/HK

Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Mar-07-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRISTINE A. POPLAWSKI & ATTY. JOSHUA WHITE, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY,
TEL. NO. 554-3878 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. EDGAR H. HAYDEN, JR. & ATTY. CHRIS STENDER OF THE HENDERSON CAVERLY PUM CHARNEY LLP, TEL. NO. (858)755-3000 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS CHRISTOPHER J. STENDER AND IMMIGRATION PRACTICE GROUP, P.C.

RULING - A R G U E D; Order To Show Cause Regarding Preliminary Injunction - THE COURT FINDS IT PROPER TO ISSUE A PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. THE PEOPLE HAVE PROVEN THAT SUFFICIENT NOTICE TO COMPLY WITH THE LAW WERE NOT GIVEN AND THERE IS A PROBABILITY OF SUCESS IN SHOWING THAT OPPOSING DEFENDANTS HAVE PARTICIPATED IN MR. GUAJARDO'S UNAUTHORIZED PRACTICE OF LAW. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed order granting Preliminary Injunction based on the transcribed order in today's hearing. THE ORDER IS STAYED FOR 10 DAYS FROM NOTICE OF ENTRY. OBJECTIONS WERE RULED ON THE RECORD. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB
